articles.title: Who Is Peter Magyar, the Man Who Toppled Hungary’s Orban
articles.summary: Mr. Magyar’s success in the Hungarian election is fueled in large part by widespread public anger at corruption and concern about sluggish economic growth.

articles.title: This Hungarian Town Explains Why Orban Could Lose on Sunday
articles.summary: Lake Balaton was beloved as a vacation spot. Now, luxury real estate projects serve many friends of Prime Minister Viktor Orban, and the locals are fed up.

articles.title: Trump Says Gas Prices Might Not Drop By Midterms, Highlighting G.O.P. Peril
articles.summary: The president has sent mixed messages on how long he expects costs to be high. Some in his party voiced fresh concern after his comments on Sunday.

articles.title: California Democrats Rushed to Back Eric Swalwell — and Quickly Abandoned Him
articles.summary: Representative Eric Swalwell, a Washington figure, was largely unknown in the California State Capitol. But influential groups began backing him for governor in the weeks before he was publicly accuse

articles.title: Eric Swalwell Suspends Campaign for California Governor After Sexual Assault Allegations
articles.summary: In a social media post, the representative said he was “deeply sorry for mistakes in judgment I’ve made in my past,” but promised to fight what he called “false allegations.”

articles.title: Fighting assault allegations, Eric Swalwell suspends his bid for California governor
articles.summary: The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.

articles.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Says Talks Failed Because U.S. Failed to Win Trust
articles.summary: Iranian officials said that while Iran had approached the negotiations in good faith, the U.S. team had not gained the Iranian delegation’s trust.

articles.title: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
articles.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.

articles.title: After failed peace talks, President Trump threatens to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Less than a day after peace talks between the US and Iran failed, President Trump said that he will blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Amid the Iran war, Ukraine makes gains against the Kremlin
articles.summary: With attention focused on Iran, Ukraine has made economic, battlefield and diplomatic gains against the Kremlin.

articles.title: Iran-US conflict fought amid censorships and digital fog of war
articles.summary: Amid the war in Iran, countries have been censoring and policing what their citizens are saying about the conflict and what information they have access to.

articles.title: Will Orbán's defeat change Hungary's relationship to the EU?
articles.summary: Historic voter turnout in Hungary ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán from power. This shift may dramatically change Hungary's relationship with the European Union, which Orbán criticized regularly.

articles.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in historic Hungary election
articles.summary: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Boja

articles.title: The consequences of Hungary's election, for Viktor Orban and the world
articles.summary: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with political scientist Abel Bojar about the results of Hungary's election, the far-reaching consequences of the vote and the future for Viktor Orban.

articles.title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán concedes defeat, ending 16 years in power
articles.summary: Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.

articles.title: How JD Vance Tried and Failed to End the War in Iran That He Opposed
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance led the highest-level talks between the United States and Iran in nearly 50 years.

articles.title: A country-by-country glance at Pope Leo XIV's trip to Africa
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.

articles.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
articles.summary: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

articles.title: The U.S. military says it will blockade Iranian ports as Iran peace talks collapse
articles.summary: After talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed, President Trump said the U.S. will "blockade" the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military said it would blockade ships entering or exiting Iranian ports.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
